items.title: Reconcile the starter framework with the intended repository
items.summary: Inspect the intended repository and adapt only repository-specific paths, commands, and factual statements.
acceptanceCriteria: Authoritative repository documents are identified and linked
Existing package scripts and framework scripts are merged without loss
No application runtime behavior is changed
Framework and available application checks are run
All reconciliation changes and unresolved conflicts are reported
evidence: Identified MattGestic/overclocked-hiit-trainer as the target repository (bundle name and CLAUDE.md content match; the other in-scope repo, MyActivities, has no package.json or comparable structure)
Merged pm:validate and pm:dashboard into the existing package.json scripts block without touching dependencies or metadata
npm run pm:validate and npm run pm:dashboard both passed
npm run lint, npm test, and npm run build all passed unchanged after adding the framework
git diff reviewed: only .claude-pm/**, CLAUDE.md, and the package.json scripts block changed

items.title: Populate the verified initial backlog
items.summary: Create outcome-focused work items from authoritative repository sources after reconciliation.
acceptanceCriteria: Each item is traceable to repository evidence or an explicit user decision
Dependencies and acceptance criteria are recorded
Speculation is labeled as an unknown rather than stated as fact
evidence: Seeded WI-0003..WI-0005 directly from docs/architecture.md's 'Explicitly deferred, flagged for confirmation' list (lines ~160-192); two adjacent items in that same list (cooldown countdown, extended programme metadata) are explicitly declined per docs/requirements.md's out-of-scope list rather than deferred, so they were intentionally not turned into backlog items — see STATUS.md risks
notes: This is an initial seed from one documented list, not an exhaustive backlog; further prioritization is a product/user decision, not something inferred here.

items.title: Swipe-to-delete / quick-swap on the block/exercise list
items.summary: A touch-gesture swipe/swap-menu component plus a live exercise-substitution data flow. Was blocked on product/design confirmation — the v3 design reference (WI-0006) now includes a working SwipeRow implementation (swipe left = delete, swipe right = quick-swap or open SwapModal), so this is unblocked as part of the v3 ActiveWorkout screen pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Delivered as part of WI-0012's ActiveWorkout/exercise-row v3 pass, reusing v3's SwipeRow interaction
notes: Confirmation source: v3 design project src/components.jsx (SwipeRow) and src/swap-modal.jsx (SwapModal), 2026-07-21.

items.title: Inline mid-run editing and a "Back" reverse-timer control
items.summary: Editable reps/weight + "confirm as actuals" during ACTIVE/RECOVER, and a reverse-timer control. Was blocked on product/design confirmation and a decision on whether in-run edits should mutate the shared programme object. The v3 reference resolves both: edits go through the same onUpdateProgram path already used elsewhere (no new mutation model), and a Back control already exists in useTimerEngine's action set to extend.
acceptanceCriteria: Delivered as part of WI-0013's Countdown/TimerRun v3 pass; needs its own timerReducer test coverage per docs/architecture.md, not a rubber-stamp
notes: Confirmation source: v3 design project src/countdown.jsx (ExerciseCard editable + confirm-as-actuals, setRunning('back')), 2026-07-21.

items.title: Per-block "inherit programme interval" override + between-blocks recovery interval
items.summary: Needs new blocks/programmes schema columns and new phase-engine behaviour. Was blocked on product/design confirmation — the v3 reference now specifies both the editor UI (per-block "inherit programme interval" checkbox + override chips) and the between-blocks concept already partially scaffolded in schema (between_blocks_seconds).
acceptanceCriteria: Delivered as part of WI-0012/WI-0013; needs new blocks.active_seconds/recover_seconds-override columns and a between-blocks phase in useTimerEngine, not just UI
notes: Confirmation source: v3 design project src/active.jsx (per-block interval override UI) and src/countdown.jsx (betweenBlocksSec handling), 2026-07-21.

items.title: Implement OVERCLOCK Refined UI v3 — screen-by-screen visual refresh
items.summary: Replicate the v3 design mockup (claude.ai/design project 019e20f9-729f-772a-8791-fac43e006909, file 'OVERCLOCK Refined UI v3.html') across every screen: Library, History, ActiveWorkout/Programme details, Countdown/TimerRun, Quick Select/Swap modal, and Toast. The mockup is a static reference (its own simplified useTimer/advance timer engine, hardcoded WeekDatePicker data, and un-wired buttons in places) — the real app's tested useTimerEngine/timerReducer, Supabase-backed data layer, and existing navigation stay as the source of truth; only the visual/interaction layer is ported. Landed as a sequence of small, always-green commits, one screen/fix at a time, per CONTRIBUTING.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Each screen visually matches the v3 reference using the app's real data (no hardcoded mock data ported in)
The four reported bugs (Library date-filter, History click-to-open, Toast obstruction, Toast palette) are fixed
npm run lint/test/build pass at every commit, not just the final one
Redundant/superseded code from the pre-v3 implementation is removed, not left dead
notes: External design reference (not a repo path): https://claude.ai/design/p/019e20f9-729f-772a-8791-fac43e006909?file=OVERCLOCK+Refined+UI+v3.html

items.title: Bug: Library week-summary date selection doesn't filter the programme list
items.summary: The WeekCalendar day cells on Library have no click handler at all — selecting a day does nothing. Should filter the Programmes list to what was actually run that day, using real session_logs data.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a day filters the Programmes list to sessions logged that day; tapping again (or a clear affordance) resets to the full list
evidence: Replaced listSessionCountsSince (counts only, insufficient) with listSessionsSince (raw started_at/programme_id rows), removing the now-redundant counts-only function

Day cells are real buttons; selecting one filters the Programmes list to programme_id's logged that day via a day->programme-ids map derived client-side from the same fetch that already feeds the day dots
Selecting the same day again, or the new clear chip next to the Programmes heading, resets to the full list; an empty state explains when nothing was run on the selected day
npm run lint/test/build all pass

items.title: Bug: History session rows aren't clickable — can't open any activity
items.summary: Session rows in the Recent Sessions list are plain divs with no onClick. programme_id also isn't selected by listAllSessions(), so there's nothing to navigate to even if a handler existed.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a session with a still-existing programme navigates to it; tapping one whose programme was deleted explains why instead of silently doing nothing
evidence: Added programme_id to listAllSessions()'s select — it was previously omitted entirely

Session rows are now real <button> elements; a row with a programme_id navigates to { screen: 'workout', programmeId }, one without (programme was deleted) shows a toast explaining why instead of silently no-oping
npm run lint/test pass

items.title: Bug: Toast notifications obstruct buttons/navigation and use an off-palette color scheme
items.summary: Toast renders top-anchored, full-width, z-index 9999, directly over each screen's header controls (Settings gear, Edit/Start, back button). Its light pink/green banner colors also read as a different visual system from the rest of the app's ink/cream/green language.
acceptanceCriteria: Toast no longer overlaps interactive controls on any screen
Toast styling reuses existing design tokens already used elsewhere in the app (ink-card treatment), not a new ad hoc palette
evidence: Repositioned to bottom (above the bottom nav) instead of top-anchored full-width; narrower max-width, moderate var(--z-toast) instead of 9999

Restyled to the ink-card treatment already used elsewhere (var(--color-bg-inverse)/--color-text-inverse/--color-border-inverse), with a small status dot (--color-timer-work success / --color-error-icon error) instead of pink/green banner backgrounds
Verified visually with a Playwright screenshot: toast no longer overlaps header EDIT/START buttons, sits cleanly above the tab bar

items.title: v3 visual refresh — History screen
items.summary: Port v3's month-calendar layout, stat tiles, and to-date/forecast/final summary mode styling onto the existing (already largely equivalent) History screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Unknown — size after WI-0007/0008 land

items.title: v3 visual refresh — ActiveWorkout + Programme details/parameters card
items.summary: Port v3's ProgramDetails card layout and ActiveWorkout header/block-list styling onto ProgrammeParameters/ActiveWorkout, reconciling with the documented out-of-scope decisions (no cooldown, no extended coach/athlete metadata).
acceptanceCriteria: Unknown — size after WI-0003/0005 scoping

items.title: v3 visual refresh — ActiveWorkout exercise rows (SwipeRow) + Swap modal
items.summary: Port v3's SwipeRow (swipe-to-delete / quick-swap / open SwapModal) onto the existing block/exercise list. Delivers WI-0003.
acceptanceCriteria: Unknown — size before starting

items.title: v3 visual refresh — Countdown/TimerRun (NEXT/THEN tiles, settings flyout, split view)
items.summary: Port v3's fullscreen countdown layout — NEXT/THEN preview tiles, consolidated settings flyout (saturated/split/volume), inline actuals editing — onto TimerRun, keeping useTimerEngine as the source of truth. Delivers part of WI-0004.
acceptanceCriteria: Unknown — size before starting; needs timerReducer test coverage for any new actions

items.title: Add a block-progress visual indicator to the workout/timer screens
items.summary: A visual component (e.g. a segmented bar or dot row) showing progress through a programme's blocks — how many blocks total and which one is current — on the Home/Library and/or TimerRun screens. Today TimerRun only renders this as a text label, not a visual indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: Check the v3 design reference and the current TimerRun/Library screens for any existing block-progress visual component before designing a new one, rather than assuming none exists
A visual indicator shows which block of the total blocks the user is on and updates live during a run
Screen placement (Library/home, TimerRun, or both) confirmed before build — not assumed
evidence: TimerRun.jsx renders only a text label (`BLOCK ${blockIndex + 1} · ROUND ${roundIndex + 1}/${block.repeat}`) — no dot/segmented visual block-progress component found in TimerRun.jsx or ActiveWorkout.jsx

docs/architecture.md's 'Adopted this pass' list for TimerRun mentions a 'tick-row round-progress indicator' for rounds within the current block, but not a cross-block progress indicator — a different concept
notes: User request 2026-07-24. Related to WI-0013's TimerRun v3 pass and WI-0011's ActiveWorkout pass — may land alongside either, not a hard dependency on either.

items.title: Programme-level "rounds per block" quick-fill control
items.summary: A control in the programme header that lets the user set a rounds value once and push it into every block's Repeat field, instead of editing each block's Repeat individually.
acceptanceCriteria: ProgrammeEditor gains a programme-level "rounds per block" input/action that writes the same value into every existing block's repeat field
Applying it is a one-off bulk-set, not a locked/linked value — per-block Repeat stays independently editable afterward
Blocks added after the bulk-set aren't silently overwritten by a stale value
evidence: ProgrammeEditor.jsx sets `repeat` per block only — each block has its own "Repeat" number input; no programme-level bulk-set control exists today
notes: User request 2026-07-24. Distinct from WI-0005 (per-block active/recover interval inherit+override) — this is about round count, not phase timing.

items.title: Extend programme type to include Weights and Not Timed
items.summary: Add "Weights" and "Not Timed" as selectable programme types alongside the existing HIIT/TABATA/CIRCUIT/AMRAP/EMOM. "Not Timed" implies a programme that doesn't run through the timer engine's countdown flow at all, which is a new run mode, not just a new label.
acceptanceCriteria: Needs product/design confirmation of what a "Not Timed" run actually is (skips TimerRun/useTimerEngine entirely vs. reuses it with countdowns hidden) before implementation is scoped
supabase/schema.sql's `programmes.type` CHECK constraint and ProgrammeEditor's type selector are updated together, not just one
Existing HIIT/TABATA/CIRCUIT/AMRAP/EMOM programmes and the timer engine are unaffected
evidence: supabase/schema.sql: `programmes.type` CHECK constraint currently only allows ('HIIT','TABATA','CIRCUIT','AMRAP','EMOM')
docs/requirements.md already documents a type selector in ProgrammeEditor for that same five-value list; "Weights" and "Not Timed" appear in neither the type list nor the out-of-scope list
notes: User request 2026-07-24. "Not Timed" has real timerReducer/phase-engine implications per docs/architecture.md and should get its own design pass, not be folded in as a simple enum addition.
